This classification indicates that Zanthoxylum aubertia is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as citrus fruits and their relatives. Within the Rutaceae family, Zanthoxylum aubertia can be further classified into the genus Zanthoxylum. The genus Zanthoxylum is a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These plants are often known for their unique aromatic properties and are used in traditional medicine.

intro: Zanthoxylum aubertia, also known as Vietnamese pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Vietnam and Southeast Asia. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Zanthoxylum aubertia is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against infections. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Zanthoxylum aubertia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as powders, capsules, and ointments.
